meta.summary: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0, including the Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview model and a new Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM focused on attentive listening, interference suppression, and more natural voice interaction. A separate strategic signal came from reports that Anthropic and SpaceX are working together on compute, pointing to continued pressure on frontier-model infrastructure supply. Most other surfaced items were early-stage GitHub tooling without enough evidence of enterprise or research impact.

featured_topics.headline: ByteDance Ships Seed 2.0 and Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview, Adds Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM
featured_topics.summary_short: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0 as a new model release. The announcement marks the formal availability of the company's latest Seed model version
featured_topics.why_it_matters: ByteDance is expanding its model stack beyond text into real-time voice interaction, a strategic move for consumer assistants, multimodal products, and China-based model competition.
key_takeaways: ByteDance Seed officially announced Seed 2.0 as a new model release through its own channels, making this a confirmed product launch rather than a rumor or benchmark leak.
The company separately released Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, signaling an intent to expose the model to public comparative evaluation and developer attention.
ByteDance also introduced a Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM designed for simultaneous conversational turn-taking, with stated emphasis on attentive listening and robust interference suppression.
The combination of Seed 2.0 plus a full-duplex speech model suggests ByteDance is targeting native voice agents and multimodal assistant experiences rather than only chatbot-style text inference.

evidence.title: Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview released on Arena
Seed 2.0 Official Launch

featured_topics.headline: Anthropic–SpaceX Compute Partnership Report Signals New Frontier AI Infrastructure Channel
featured_topics.summary_short: A report says Anthropic and SpaceX have become compute partners in an unexpected infrastructure relationship. The item frames the partnership as a notable development in AI compute supply and deployment
featured_topics.why_it_matters: If confirmed, a compute relationship between Anthropic and SpaceX would show leading model labs broadening beyond hyperscaler dependence, with implications for supply security and bargaining power.
key_takeaways: The reported partnership centers on compute access, making it strategically important in a market where GPU availability and power-constrained data center capacity remain major bottlenecks.
Anthropic is one of the highest-profile frontier model developers, so any shift in its infrastructure sourcing could influence how other labs structure cloud and training partnerships.
SpaceX appearing in the AI compute stack would be notable because it expands the set of nontraditional infrastructure players participating in model training and deployment economics.

evidence.title: Anthropic, SpaceX(AI) become unlikely compute partners
